explicit_text: I joined the United States Marine Corps Reserve back in March of 1950 while I was still in high school. Honestly, I joined mainly to play basketball since the Marine Corps Reserve had a team, and I wasn't quite good enough to make my high school team. About six of us from my class joined together, and soon after, our unit was activated for active duty during the Korean War. Being part of the Reserves was a big part of my life, and it led me directly into my service overseas.
implicit_text: I joined the Marine Corps Reserve back in March of 1950 while I was still in high school. Honestly, I joined mainly to play basketball since the reserve unit had a team, and I wasn't quite good enough to make my high school team. About six of us from my class joined together, and soon after, our unit was activated for active duty during the conflict in Korea. Being part of the reserves was a big part of my life, and it led me directly into my service overseas.

explicit_text: The Korean War started in June 1950, and by mid-July, my Marine Corps Reserve unit was called to active duty. I was only 17 when I was sent to Korea, where I served for 11 months in a heavy weapons company. Initially trained on 3.5 rockets, we quickly switched to machine guns because the terrain was mountainous and tanks were virtually nonexistent. During my time there, I supported infantry rifle companies and was fortunate that none of my fellow reservists were wounded or killed, though we did lose one Army friend.
implicit_text: The conflict in Korea started in June 1950, and by mid-July, my Marine Corps Reserve unit was called to active duty. I was only 17 when I was sent to the peninsula, where I served for 11 months in a heavy weapons company. Initially trained on 3.5 rockets, we quickly switched to machine guns because the terrain was mountainous and tanks were virtually nonexistent. During my time there, I supported infantry rifle companies and was fortunate that none of my fellow reservists were wounded or killed, though we did lose one Army friend.
